summary: Small data/string matching PR for `src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c`. It populated the `grI1_803E4950` stage-data callback table with real callback symbols instead of `NULL`, adjusted exact diagnostic/assert strings, removed use of a standalone `grinishie1.c` global string in favor of a literal, and changed one assert label from `new_gobj` to `map_gobj`. The automated decomp.dev report showed `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` `.data` improving from 55.52% to 89.65% (+139 bytes), plus a tiny `grInishie1_801FB3F0` improvement from 80.43% to 80.44%.

key_files.role: Only changed file; stage data initializer, diagnostic strings, and assert text were adjusted for better matching.

what_changed: Filled `grI1_803E4950.stage_data` slots that were previously `NULL` with `grI1_803E48F4`, `grInishie1_801FA90C`, `grInishie1_801FA908`, `grInishie1_801FA984`, `grInishie1_801FA988`, `grInishie1_801FA9AC`, `grInishie1_801FCBB0`, and `grInishie1_801FCBB8`.
Changed the `invalid_state(line)` `OSReport` text from `%s:%d: oioi...` with file string `grinishie1.c` to `%s:%d: oioi..\n` with string argument `i!=HATENA_APPEAR_CHECKLOOP`.
Commented out `char grI1_803E49A8[] = "grinishie1.c";` and changed `grInishie1_801FA9B4` to pass the literal `"grinishie1.c"` directly to `OSReport`.
Updated the `grInishie1_801FB3F0_inline` assert message from `%s:%d: oioi...` to `%s:%d: oioi..\n`.
Updated the `fn_801FBF6C` null-gobj assert string from `new_gobj` to `map_gobj`.
Net diff was limited to one C file with 13 additions and 13 deletions across 6 hunks.
smart_moves: Targeted the stage-data initializer directly: replacing placeholder `NULL` entries with known callback pointers is a high-leverage way to improve `.data` matching without rewriting behavior.
Matched diagnostic string bytes exactly, including the number of dots, embedded newline, and assert label; these tiny rodata/data details materially affected match quality.
Avoided an artificial global `char[]` for `"grinishie1.c"` where the diff suggests the original layout wanted a string literal at the call site instead.
Kept the patch narrow and evidence-driven; the decomp.dev bot confirmed a large `.data` gain for the same unit.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, a `StageData` object with many `NULL` lifecycle/callback slots may be a matching placeholder rather than true runtime absence; check nearby `StageCallbacks` arrays and known stage entry points before assuming NULL is correct.
String storage class matters for data matching: `char symbol[] = "..."` emits a data object, while a direct string literal can change section placement and deduplication behavior.
Assert and `OSReport` strings should preserve retail spelling, punctuation, and newlines exactly; even `oioi...` versus `oioi..\n` or `new_gobj` versus `map_gobj` can affect matching.
Automated match reports are useful for validating data-layout work: here the main measurable win was `.data`, not a large function-code rewrite.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved existing address-suffixed placeholder names such as `grInishie1_801FA90C`, `grInishie1_801FCBB0`, and `grI1_803E4950`; no semantic renames were attempted.
Data symbols in this file use the short `grI1_803E....` form, while functions use `grInishie1_80......`; future changes should keep that distinction unless a broader rename is justified.
Retail diagnostic text may contain nonstandard wording or typos, e.g. `couldn t get gobj`, `oioi`, and `i!=HATENA_APPEAR_CHECKLOOP`; preserve exact text when matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use full initializer pointer tables instead of `NULL` placeholders to materialize the expected `.data` pointer values.
Prefer the string representation that matches emitted storage: direct literals can be better than named `char[]` globals when the original data section lacks the standalone symbol.
Audit assert/OSReport string literals byte-for-byte, including trailing `\n`, because these influence rodata/data layout and sometimes nearby inline-function matching.
The `grInishie1_801FB3F0_inline` string tweak corresponded to a small reported improvement in `grInishie1_801FB3F0` despite no substantive control-flow change shown in the diff.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or review approvals were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev comment for GALE01 reported 2 improvements: `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` `.data` +139 bytes, 55.52% to 89.65%; `grInishie1_801FB3F0` 80.43% to 80.44%.
follow_up_queries: Find other `src/melee/gr/*.c` stage files where `StageData` callback slots are still `NULL` despite nearby `StageCallbacks` arrays or known lifecycle functions.
Search for standalone `char[] = "<file>.c"` globals used only in `OSReport` or `__assert`; verify whether retail used a named data object or a direct string literal.
Audit remaining `grinishie1.c` diagnostic strings against retail bytes, especially assert labels, punctuation, and newline placement.
Check whether the commented-out `grI1_803E49A8` line should eventually be removed or replaced with a clearer matching comment if project style prefers no dead commented declarations.
